violations.rule: B.10 - Token estimation logic must be isolated into TokenEstimator module
violations.reason: Token estimation logic (estimate_tokens, clean_source, remove_comments, remove_unused_imports) is embedded directly in ContextOptimizer class rather than isolated in a separate TokenEstimator module. This violates separation of concerns and makes token estimation logic non-swappable.
violations.refactor: Create app/context_optimizer/token_estimator.py with TokenEstimator class containing estimate_tokens(), clean_source(), remove_comments(), remove_unused_imports() methods. ContextOptimizer should accept TokenEstimator as dependency injection parameter. This enables swapping token estimation strategies (e.g., tiktoken, sentencepiece) without modifying optimizer logic.

violations.rule: A.5 - No circular dependency edges unless explicitly annotated
violations.reason: GraphBuilder._detect_cycles() detects and logs circular dependencies but does not annotate them in the graph structure. Cycles are logged but not marked with metadata (e.g., edge attribute 'is_circular': True) that downstream consumers could use.
violations.refactor: When cycles are detected, annotate circular edges with metadata: self.graph.edges[source, target]['is_circular'] = True. This allows downstream modules to handle circular dependencies explicitly rather than discovering them through traversal.

violations.rule: C.16 - All modules must expose interfaces for swapping implementations
violations.reason: DependencyGraph class in builder.py is a stub with pass statements. Methods add_node(), add_edge(), get_dependencies() are not implemented. This appears to be dead code or incomplete interface definition.
violations.refactor: Either: (1) Remove builder.py if it's unused dead code, OR (2) Define it as an abstract base class (ABC) with @abstractmethod decorators to serve as swappable interface for GraphBuilder, OR (3) Complete the implementation if it serves a different purpose than GraphBuilder.

cross_layer_issues: Translation module (app/translation/orchestrator.py) imports from app.parsers.base (ASTNode) - This is ACCEPTABLE as ASTNode is a data structure, not business logic
Translation module imports from app.context_optimizer.optimizer (ContextOptimizer) - This is ACCEPTABLE as translation is a higher layer consuming optimizer services
No violations: Context optimizer does NOT import from ingestion or translation modules
No violations: Dependency graph builder does NOT import from translation, prompting, or optimizer modules

risk_factors: Token estimation uses naive heuristic (4 chars per token) rather than proper tokenizer
No validation that combined_source stays under Gemini's actual context window
Gemini 1.5 Flash has 1M token context window, but config default is 100K
estimate_tokens() method is placeholder - production should use tiktoken or model-specific tokenizer
recommendations: Integrate proper tokenizer (tiktoken for GPT-style, sentencepiece for others)
Add safety margin (e.g., 90% of max_tokens) to account for estimation errors
Validate against model-specific limits before LLM API calls
Log actual token usage from LLM responses to calibrate estimator

dependency_graph_builder.rule_1_ast_only: PASS - GraphBuilder.build_graph() accepts only List[ASTNode], no file I/O
dependency_graph_builder.rule_2_no_direct_file_reads: PASS - No open(), read(), or Path operations found in dependency_graph module
dependency_graph_builder.rule_3_no_translation_calls: PASS - No imports from app.translation, app.llm, or app.prompt_versioning
dependency_graph_builder.rule_4_graph_schema: PASS - Graph includes node_id, file_path, name, node_type, start_line, end_line
dependency_graph_builder.rule_5_circular_annotation: FAIL - Cycles detected but not annotated in graph structure

context_optimizer.rule_6_depends_only_on_graph: PASS - Only imports from app.parsers.base (ASTNode), app.core.config, app.core.logging
context_optimizer.rule_7_no_llm_calls: PASS - No LLM API calls found (no .generate(), .complete(), requests.post)
context_optimizer.rule_8_configurable_depth: PASS - expansion_depth configurable via __init__ parameter and config.CONTEXT_EXPANSION_DEPTH
context_optimizer.rule_9_logs_included_excluded: PASS - Logs included_nodes and excluded_nodes in optimize_context()
context_optimizer.rule_10_token_estimator_isolated: FAIL - Token estimation embedded in ContextOptimizer, not separate module
context_optimizer.rule_11_no_hardcoded_limits: PASS - No hardcoded token limits, uses config.MAX_TOKEN_LIMIT
context_optimizer.rule_12_no_ingestion_translation_access: PASS - No imports from app.ingestion or app.translation

cross_layer_integrity.rule_13_no_boundary_violations: PASS - No cross-layer imports violating architecture
cross_layer_integrity.rule_14_no_optimizer_to_translation: PASS - Context optimizer does not call translation modules
cross_layer_integrity.rule_15_no_ast_mutation: PASS - No AST field assignments found in optimizer or graph builder
cross_layer_integrity.rule_16_swappable_interfaces: PARTIAL - GraphBuilder exposes interface, but DependencyGraph stub is incomplete

architectural_strengths: Clean separation: GraphBuilder only consumes AST nodes, never reads files directly
Proper layering: Context optimizer depends only on dependency_graph, not on translation/ingestion
Configurable parameters: Token limits and expansion depth come from config file, not hardcoded
Immutable AST: No mutations detected - ASTNode objects are read-only throughout pipeline
Logging discipline: Both modules log included/excluded nodes and graph statistics
BFS traversal: Proper depth-limited dependency expansion with configurable limits
Error handling: Proper exceptions (EmptyGraphError, MissingNodeError, TokenLimitExceededError)
No LLM coupling: Context optimizer has zero LLM API dependencies
architectural_weaknesses: Token estimation is naive placeholder (4 chars/token heuristic)
No separate TokenEstimator module - violates single responsibility principle
Circular dependencies detected but not annotated for downstream consumers
DependencyGraph class in builder.py is incomplete stub (dead code or unfinished interface)
No validation that optimized context fits within actual model context window
Token estimation methods (clean_source, remove_comments) are language-agnostic placeholders

recommendations.rationale: Token estimation logic should be isolated for swappability and testing. Different LLM providers use different tokenizers (tiktoken for OpenAI, sentencepiece for others).
recommendations.implementation: Create app/context_optimizer/token_estimator.py with abstract TokenEstimator base class and concrete implementations (HeuristicTokenEstimator, TiktokenEstimator). Inject into ContextOptimizer via constructor.

recommendations.rationale: Downstream modules need to know which edges are circular to avoid infinite traversal or handle them specially during translation.
recommendations.implementation: In GraphBuilder._detect_cycles(), after detecting cycles, iterate through cycle nodes and mark edges: for cycle in cycles: for i in range(len(cycle)): source, target = cycle[i], cycle[(i+1) % len(cycle)]; self.graph.edges[source, target]['is_circular'] = True

recommendations.rationale: Naive 4-char heuristic can be off by 50%+. Use tiktoken for GPT-style models or model-specific tokenizer.
recommendations.implementation: Add tiktoken dependency, create TiktokenEstimator class, use encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4') for accurate token counts.

recommendations.rationale: Incomplete class creates confusion. Either remove dead code or complete the interface.
recommendations.implementation: If unused, delete builder.py. If intended as interface, convert to ABC with @abstractmethod decorators. If different purpose, document and implement.

recommendations.rationale: Validate optimized context fits within model's actual limit before LLM call to fail fast.
recommendations.implementation: In optimize_context(), after building result, validate: if result.estimated_tokens > model_context_limit: raise ContextWindowExceededError(). Get model_context_limit from config or model metadata.

test_coverage_notes.graph_builder_tests: tests/test_graph_builder.py exists with 20 tests covering build_graph, subgraph extraction, cycle detection
test_coverage_notes.context_optimizer_tests: tests/test_context_optimizer.py exists covering optimize_context, token limits, depth limits
missing_tests: No tests for circular dependency annotation
No tests for TokenEstimator as separate module (doesn't exist yet)
No tests validating token estimation accuracy against real tokenizer

security_notes.no_file_injection: PASS - No direct file reads that could be exploited
security_notes.no_code_execution: PASS - No eval(), exec(), or dynamic code execution in optimizer/graph builder
security_notes.input_validation: PASS - Proper validation of target_node_id, depth parameters
security_notes.dos_protection: PASS - Depth limits and token limits prevent infinite expansion

performance_notes.graph_complexity: O(V + E) for graph construction where V=nodes, E=edges
performance_notes.bfs_complexity: O(V + E) for subgraph extraction with depth limit
performance_notes.optimization_complexity: O(D * B) where D=depth, B=branching factor (dependencies per node)
bottlenecks: Token estimation via string operations (clean_source, remove_comments) is O(n) where n=source length
Regex-based comment removal is naive and could be slow on large files
